name: Tea (food medium) — brewed black tea infusion, unsweetened
basis: per 100 g brewed infusion (~100 mL), unsweetened black tea prepared from ~1 g leaf / 100 mL tap water

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 171917 (Beverages, tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water)
components.evidence: Brewed tea is ~99.7% water; the medium is essentially dilute water.

components.name: total carbohydrate
components.evidence: Total carbohydrate 0.3 g/100 g; almost no digestible sugar in the infusion.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: USDA reports ~0 g total sugars; only trace free sugars leach into the infusion (see individual sugars).

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823
components.evidence: Trace free glucose present in tea leaf/infusion but not quantified at infusion strength; growth-negligible.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Trace free fructose in tea; growth-negligible in the infusion.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor sucrose in leaf; only trace carries into infusion. Present in current record; retained at trace.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Yu et al. 2023 Molecules 28:2339 (review) / Zhang et al. 2022 doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823
components.evidence: Quinic acid is the dominant organic acid of tea (6-9 mg/g dry leaf); ~7 mg/100 g infusion at ~1 g leaf/100 mL. MISSING from original FooDB record.

components.name: gallic acid
components.source: Baruah & Mahanta / Chandini et al. 2015 PMC4520228 (max extractable 8 mg/g leaf)
components.evidence: Gallic acid extracts to ~6-8 mg/100 g infusion; already present in record, now quantified.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: HealWithFood/USDA oxalate data; 5-30 mg per 200 mL cup
components.evidence: ~4-16 mg oxalate per 200 mL cup -> ~5 mg/100 g infusion. MISSING from original record.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823; Yu et al. 2023 Molecules 28:2339
components.evidence: Malic acid a minor tea organic acid; low mg/100 g in infusion. MISSING from original record.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Citric acid present at low levels in tea infusion. MISSING from original record.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823; Yu et al. 2023 Molecules 28:2339
components.evidence: Minor organic acid of tea. MISSING from original record.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.evidence: Present in tea (shikimate pathway precursor of gallic acid); unquantified at infusion strength.

components.name: L-theanine
components.source: Keenan et al. 2011 Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2010.10.049; PMC12248710
components.evidence: Dominant free amino acid / organic-N source of tea; ~12-24 mg per 200 mL cup -> ~11 mg/100 g. Already in uncovered list.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: FooDB FOOD00038; Yu et al. 2023 Molecules 28:2339
components.evidence: Present as free amino acid at low mg/100 g; second most abundant free AA after theanine.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid; total protein ~0, so all AA are minor.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid in tea infusion.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid (record listed D-Serine; L-serine is the physiological form).

components.name: glycine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: Trace free amino acid.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Trace; sulfur amino acid.

components.name: caffeine
components.evidence: ~20 mg/100 g (200 mg/L); defining tea alkaloid, not a general growth substrate but a major dissolved metabolite.

components.name: theobromine
components.evidence: Minor purine alkaloid of tea.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral of the infusion (37 mg/100 g).

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: 1 mg/100 g; low P is growth-limiting in tea as a medium.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: 0.22 mg/100 g; tea is a notable Mn accumulator, the most abundant trace metal of the infusion.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: ~0 mg/100 g in tap-water brew.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: 0.02 mg/100 g. Original record had 3 redundant iron entries; consolidated to one.

components.name: fluoride
components.source: USDA FDC 171917; Malinowska/Whitford; ARS jfca_teafluoride (373+/-49 ug/100 g caffeinated brewed tea)
components.evidence: Tea is a strong fluoride accumulator; ~3.7 mg/L. Genuine defining trace ion of tea.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: 0.014 mg/100 g; only measurable vitamin.

components.name: vitamin B6 (pyridoxine)
components.evidence: ~0.001 mg/100 g; trace.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: ~5 ug DFE/100 g; trace.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: ~0 mg/100 g in brewed black tea.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Minor; present in record, unquantified at infusion strength.

components.name: methylamine
components.evidence: Genuine trace volatile amine of tea.

components.name: S-methyl-L-methionine
components.evidence: Genuine minor sulfur compound of tea leaf.

components.name: hexanal
components.evidence: Genuine trace aroma aldehyde; growth-negligible but real.

components.name: heptanal
components.evidence: Genuine trace aroma aldehyde.

components.name: nonanal
components.evidence: Genuine trace aroma aldehyde.

components.name: quercetin
components.evidence: Genuine tea flavonol (largely as glycosides rutin/hyperin).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Genuine plant carotenoid of tea leaf; low carry-over to infusion.

components.name: alpha-tocopherol
components.evidence: Leaf-associated vitamin E; minimal in aqueous infusion.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Present in fresh leaf; largely degraded in black (oxidised) tea infusion.

components.name: nitrogen (N2)
components.evidence: Standard medium exchange metabolite; retained.

components.name: O2
components.evidence: Standard aerobic exchange; retained.

components.name: CO2
components.evidence: Standard exchange; retained.

components.name: H2O
components.evidence: Solvent/exchange; retained.

components.name: H+
components.evidence: Proton exchange; retained. Tea infusion pH ~4.9-5.5.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Trace inorganic nitrogen; retained.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Trace sulfur source; retained.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Trace anion; retained.

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: Synthetic contraceptive-drug steroid; biologically implausible in a plant infusion. FooDB predicted/contaminant noise.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Animal-form vitamin A; a plant food (tea) supplies beta-carotene, not preformed retinol. FooDB implausible entry.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Apiaceae/citrus furanocoumarin, not a tea constituent; FooDB mis-assignment/predicted, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Aluminum / Aluminum
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace metal; tea accumulates Al but it is not a growth nutrient. Per curation rule drop aluminium.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal; not a growth nutrient of tea at relevant levels.

remove.name: Co2+ (cobalt ion)
remove.reason: Trace contaminant metal; tea B12/cobalamin is ~0 so cobalt has no growth role here. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Trace element with no measured relevant level in brewed tea; retain only if the model requires a Mo source (borderline noise).

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
remove.reason: Redundant/compartment-artifact iron entries; consolidated into a single 'iron' component (0.02 mg/100 g).

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: Brewed tea infusion contains essentially no starch; leaf starch does not solubilise. Implausible growth substrate at infusion strength.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace metal with no meaningful measured level in the infusion; FooDB noise.

missing_added: quinic acid (dominant organic acid of tea)
keep_uncovered: L-theanine (also listed as component; dominant tea amino acid, may lack BiGG met)
geraniol, nerolidol, linalool oxides, alpha-ionone, hexenals/hexenols (genuine tea volatiles)
sources: USDA FDC 171917 (Beverages, tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water, SR Legacy)
Yu, Yang et al. 2023, Molecules 28:2339 doi:10.3390/molecules28052339 (organic acids of tea review)
Zhang et al. 2022, Food Science & Nutrition doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823 (organic acids sour taste black vs green tea)
Chandini/Rajalakshmi et al. 2015, PMC4520228 (extraction kinetics: gallic acid 8 mg/g, EGCG 11.8 mg/g, caffeine 42 mg/g leaf)
Keenan et al. 2011, Food Chemistry doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2010.10.049 (theanine ~24 mg/200 mL cup)
USDA ARS jfca_teafluoride / Malinowska (fluoride 373+/-49 ug/100 g brewed black tea)
notes: Interpreted as unsweetened brewed BLACK TEA INFUSION (~100 mL), the form a microbe would grow in; it is an extremely dilute, nutrient-poor medium (~99.7% water). No meaningful bulk sugar (<0.3 g/100 g carbohydrate, ~0 free sugar), so the DOMINANT carbon/energy substrates are organic acids (quinic > gallic > oxalic > malic/citric/succinic) plus soluble polyphenols (catechins/theaflavins/thearubigins), and the dominant organic-N source is L-theanine with minor free amino acids; total protein ~0. Potassium is the main mineral; fluoride and manganese are the defining trace ions. Phosphorus (1 mg/100 g) is strongly growth-limiting. Removed FooDB noise = a synthetic estrogen drug, animal retinol, an Apiaceae furanocoumarin, and non-nutritive trace metals (Al, Ni, Co, Cr, Mo).
